purpose: Identifies mutually contradictory inflation-path assumptions embedded across asset classes to find tradeable mispricings and determine the dominant portfolio construction variable
components: Equity multiples and implied inflation/discount rate path
Credit spreads (IG OAS 90-110bp, HY 300-350bp) and embedded disinflationary probability (65-70%)
Breakeven inflation levels and risk premium
Real yields and implied equilibrium rate
Maturity-dependent correlation bifurcation (2Y negative, 30Y positive) as fiscal-dominance fingerprint
Non-default spread component as regime indicator (expanded from 60-70% to 75-85% of IG OAS)
current_assessment: Asset classes embed contradictory assumptions. Credit spreads price a disinflationary glide path inconsistent with structural persistence. Breakevens underprice right-tail risk. Inflation optionality offers asymmetric risk/reward regardless of point-estimate debates. Inflation regime identification is the dominant portfolio construction variable.
limitations: TIPS liquidity premium uncertainty (-80bp to +40bp) makes breakeven level claims fragile
Scenario probability weights are inherently subjective (Brier scores of 0.3-0.4 at 12+ month horizons)
Cross-asset trades require the regime to resolve within the trade horizon
Specific alpha estimates do not survive scrutiny even when directional finding does
